game_description: Build an ancient civilization through three ages of card drafting.

strategies.condition: Choosing which side of wonder board
strategies.strategy_detailed: Competitive top-tier wonders: Babylon B (play the normally-discarded 7th card each age), Halikarnassos B (build from discards twice — stages 1 and 3), Olympia A (free build per age, stage 2), Rhodes A (military + cheap stages). Side B isn't universally better; Olympia and Rhodes both favor Side A.
strategy_stealth: Olympia A, Rhodes A also S-tier

strategies.strategy_detailed: Manufactured goods (gray: glass, papyrus, textile) are scarcer than raw (brown) and gate the most valuable Age II/III cards. Audit your wonder's resources, then plan to draft the gray you lack in Age I. Self-sufficient bases eliminate coin tax on neighbors and immunize you from price gouging.

Lock at least one gray (manufactured) in Age I — they're scarcer and required for high-VP Age II/III cards. One gray plus one brown covers most Age II costs. Take a science card only if you'll commit fully — partial science (1-2 symbols) scores poorly versus its opportunity cost.

strategies.condition: Left neighbor building shields in Age I
strategies.strategy_detailed: Conflict tokens pay +1/+3/+5 per age won, −1 per age lost (per neighbor). Match left neighbor's shield count exactly each age or pivot fully to VP. Half-measures (1 shield versus their 2) waste a pick and still lose. If they show 2 shields in Age I, plan a 3-shield card in Age II.
strategy_stealth: Match left shields exactly per age

strategies.strategy_detailed: Weak-military left neighbor is free Conflict points. Build exactly one shield per age to win the comparison (+1/+3/+5 = +9 total), then spend remaining picks on your core strategy. Over-investing past the minimum shield count wastes draft tempo on tokens you already secured.
strategy_stealth: 1 shield per age beats weak left

strategies.condition: Right neighbor building shields in Age I
strategies.strategy_detailed: Military scores against both neighbors every age — right side counts equally. Match right neighbor's shield count, prioritizing Age II/III where swings are larger (+3 and +5). Right is harder to read since drafts flow away from you in Ages I/III; rely on visible board.
strategy_stealth: Right side scores equally per age
Match right shields Age II/III priority
Read visible board, not draft flow

strategies.strategy_detailed: One shield per age beats a non-military right neighbor for +9 VP across the game. Combined with a weak left, total military VP can reach +18 with just 3 shield cards — among the best VP-per-pick ratios in the game.

strategies.condition: Science path viable in Age I
strategies.strategy_detailed: Science scores n² per symbol plus 7 per complete triplet. Break-even commitment is ~6 cards (2-2-2 = 12 + 7 = 19 VP). Take science yourself when committed — passing it to a neighbor who picks before you on the next rotation is the most common failure mode.

strategies.condition: Completing a wonder stage in Age II
strategies.strategy_detailed: Wonder stages convert weak draft picks into VP, resources, or abilities that cascade into Age III. Bury a low-value card to advance when the stage yields 3+ VP or an ability. Exception: never bury a card that directly denies a neighbor's strategy — denial value often exceeds the stage's VP.
strategy_stealth: Bury weak cards to advance stage

strategies.condition: Strong guild card available in Age III
strategies.strategy_detailed: Most guilds score on NEIGHBORS' achievements — assess neighbors first. Workers/Craftsmen/Traders/Philosophers/Magistrates score on neighbor color counts; Spies on neighbor defeats. Exceptions on YOUR cards: Shipowners and Scientists. Bury via wonder to deny a strong guild to downstream neighbor.

strategies.condition: Low on coins in Age II
strategies.strategy_detailed: Vineyard (1 coin/brown in row) and Bazaar (2 coins/gray in row) generate coins per row's resource cards. Trading Posts (East/West/Marketplace) cut neighbor-purchase cost from 2 to 1 coin for the matching resource. Coins matter less for endgame VP (1 per 3) than for routine trades.

Before final Age III picks, count exact shields on both neighbors. A last-pick shield card swinging Age III from −1 to +5 is a 6 VP gain on one side — usually more than any single blue card. Compute the swing on both sides; sometimes one shield wins two comparisons.
strategy_stealth: One shield can win both sides

strategies.condition: Tied on points entering final picks
strategies.strategy_detailed: Official tiebreaker is most coins (1 VP per 3 already counted; raw coin count breaks ties beyond that). Maximize coins in the final picks if scores look close. A Bazaar-style commercial card or unused Age III commercial drafted late can swing a tiebreaker without giving up VP elsewhere.

strategies.condition: Completing a science triplet in Age III
strategies.strategy_detailed: Completing a third symbol of any type adds 7 VP (set bonus) plus the n² jump (e.g., 2² to 3² = +5). A single triplet-completing card is worth 12+ VP — draft it over almost anything except game-deciding military shields or guilds worth 15+.
